---
title: EMBL
alias: &ALIAS embl
href: https://www.embl.org/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

The European Molecular Biology Laboratory (EMBL) is Europe's flagship laboratory for the life sciences, providing open-access molecular biology databases and bioinformatics services.

## Key Features

- Operates EMBL-EBI, hosting databases including UniProt, Ensembl, ArrayExpress, and ChEMBL
- Provides open-access genomic, proteomic, and chemical biology data with standardized formats
- Offers training programs, workshops, and open educational resources in bioinformatics
- Supports interoperability through adherence to FAIR data principles and community standards
- Maintains the European Nucleotide Archive (ENA) as part of INSDC data sharing

## Use Cases in Research

- Accessing annotated European nucleotide sequences and genome assemblies via ENA
- Querying integrated protein function, structure, and interaction data across EMBL-EBI resources
- Using EMBL bioinformatics tools for sequence analysis, variant annotation, and pathway enrichment
- Linking chemical bioactivity data (ChEMBL) to genomic targets for drug discovery workflows
- Training in FAIR data management practices for microbiome and genomic research projects

## Getting Started

Visit [EMBL](https://www.embl.org/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
